title: SSC CGL T-I Reasoning Section Test

questions.question: If 1 is added for each even digit and 1 is subtracted from each odd digit of the number 56218643, how many digits are repeated more than once in the new number thus formed?
questions.explanation: Information Given: Number = 56218643 Rule: +1 to even digits; −1 to odd digits Modify each digit and check repetition. 5 (odd) → 4 6 (even) → 7 2 (even) → 3 1 (odd) → 0 8 (even) → 9 6 (even) → 7 4 (even) → 5 3 (odd) → 2 New number: 4 7 3 0 9 7 5 2 Digit 7 appears twice. Only one digit is repeated more than once. Final Answer: One Final Correct Option: (D)

questions.question: KI 8 is related to IE -8 in a certain way. In the same way, HJ 9 is related to FF -7. To which of the following is QQ 15 related, following the same logic?
questions.explanation: Information Given: KI 8 → IE −8 HJ 9 → FF −7 Logic given: New number = Original number − 16 Logic:Letters: −2, −4 Number: −16 Explanation: KI 8: K −2 = I I −4 = E 8 −16 = −8 ✔ HJ 9: H −2 = F J −4 = F 9 −16 = −7 ✔ Now QQ 15: Q −2 = O Q −4 = M 15 −16 = −1 So result = OM −1 Final Answer: OM −1 Final Correct Option: (D)

questions.question: A statement is given followed by two conclusions. Find which conclusion is TRUE. Statement: P ≥ Q; R = S ≤ T; Q ≥ R; T ≥ P Conclusions: I. P ≥ R II. S ≤ Q ​​
questions.explanation: Statement: P ≥ Q; R = S ≤ T; Q ≥ R; T ≥ P Conclusions: I. P ≥ R From the statements: P ≥ Q and Q ≥ R So, P ≥ Q ≥ R => P ≥ R Conclusion I is TRUE II. S ≤ Q We know: R = S and Q ≥ R So, Q ≥ R = S => S ≤ Q Conclusion II is TRUE So, Both I and II Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: ​Select the combination of numbers so that the letters arranged accordingly will form a meaningful word. ​R, S, P, A, I
questions.explanation: Rearrange the letters R, S, P, A, I to form a meaningful word. A valid arrangement is P A R I S → PARIS. It uses all given letters exactly once: P, A, R, I, S PARIS is a well-known city (capital of France), so the correct category is City. Why the other options are incorrect (B) River: No famous river name can be formed with exactly these letters (R, S, P, A, I). (C) Mountain: These letters don’t rearrange into a recognized mountain name. (D) Animal: Words like asp or aspis don’t fit exactly—asp leaves unused letters (R, I), and aspis needs two S’s but we have only one.

questions.question: Which item does not belong to the group?
questions.explanation: Violin, Drums and Trumpet are musical instruments. Screwdriver is a tool, not a musical instrument. So, Screwdriver is odd one out. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: How many triangles are there in the given figure? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/10499ff0-2260-40fe-9ae7-67d6fc7d45b7.png) ​
questions.explanation: There are 10 triangles given below in the figure. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/787bb30c-c7bf-436e-a697-5c13899d358c.png) Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: A piece of paper is placed as shown in the figure below, a part of which is missing. From the given figures, select the appropriate missing part. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/7cfa772d-555e-41b9-9fb2-185f92d1154e.png)
questions.explanation: The missing figure that will complete the figure is shown below. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/25950aa6-ee00-4577-988d-48f5ed194b4f.png) Thus, the correct option is: (c)

questions.question: Mr. Toni ranked 94th from the bottom and 65th from the top in his class. How many students are there in his class?
questions.explanation: Given: Mr. Toni ranked 94th from the bottom and 65th from the top in his class. Formula: Total students = (Rank from top) + (Rank from bottom) − 1 = 65 + 94 − 1 = 159 − 1 = 158 So, 158 students are there in his class. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: In the following question, select the number which can be placed at the sign of (?) from the given alternatives. 72 63 54 80 72 63 52 45 ?
questions.explanation: Logic: Column 1 − (sum of digits of Column 1) = Column 2 Similarly, Column 2 − (sum of digits of Column 2) = Column 3 Check row-wise. First row: Column 1 = 72 Sum of digits of 72 = 7 + 2 = 9 72 − 9 = 63 (Column 2) Column 2 = 63 Sum of digits of 63 = 6 + 3 = 9 63 − 9 = 54 (Column 3) Second row: Column 1 = 80 Sum of digits of 80 = 8 + 0 = 8 80 − 8 = 72 (Column 2) Column 2 = 72 Sum of digits of 72 = 7 + 2 = 9 72 − 9 = 63 (Column 3) Third row: Column 1 = 52 Sum of digits of 52 = 5 + 2 = 7 52 − 7 = 45 (Column 2) Column 2 = 45 Sum of digits of 45 = 4 + 5 = 9 45 − 9 = 36 Required number at (?) is 36

questions.question: Seven boxes I, J, K, L, M, N and P are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only L is kept above M. Only P is kept between M and J. Only N is kept below K. How many boxes are kept between I and N?
questions.explanation: Given: ​Seven boxes I, J, K, L, M, N and P are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only L is kept above M. Only P is kept between M and J. Only N is kept below K. Position (Top to Bottom) Box 1 L 2 M 3 P 4 J 5 I 6 K 7 N So, one box is kept between I and N. Thus, the correct option is: (b)

questions.question: ​The sequence of folding a piece of paper and the manner in which the folded paper has been cut is shown below. Choose the figure that would most closely resemble the unfolded form of the paper. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/bcf1efbb-1fea-48b6-99d6-45fea9d9a5aa.png) ​
questions.explanation: After unfolding we get: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/b04fb17a-0665-484e-ae59-4544ab132543.png) Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: The question below contains two assumptions I and II followed by the statement. You have to accept everything given in the statement as true and then consider both the assumptions and decide which of them is/are logically follow/s the information given in the statement beyond a reasonable doubt? Statements: Nowadays RO technology is used to purify water. Assumption: I. The use of an artificial technique is hazardous to health. II. People are ready to adopt modern technology. ​​
options: Neither Assumptions I nor II are implicit.
Both Assumptions I and II are implicit.
questions.explanation: Statements: Nowadays RO technology is used to purify water. Assumption: I. The use of an artificial technique is hazardous to health. This is not implied. The statement does not suggest any health hazard. II. People are ready to adopt modern technology. ​​ This is implicit, because widespread use of RO technology is possible only if people accept and adopt it. So, Only Assumption II is implicit. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: If P @ Q = P² + Q, then what is the value of 6 @ 5?
questions.explanation: Given: If P @ Q = P2​+ Q = 6 @ 5 = 62​+ 5 = 36 + 5 = 41 So, 41 is the value of 6 @ 5. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: In a certain code language: If β α λ λ is coded as BALL and κ ε ψ is coded as KEY, then what is α κ λ ?
questions.explanation: Given: In a certain code language: If β α λ λ is coded as BALL and κ ε ψ is coded as KEY. Logic: Direct coding is given. For, β α λ λ - BALL β = B, α = A, λ = L, λ = L For, κ ε ψ - KEY κ = K, ε = E, ψ = Y Similarly, α κ λ - ? α = A, κ = K, λ = L So, α κ λ is coded as AKL. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: In the Venn diagram given below, circle ‘P’ represents educated, circle ‘Q’ represents rural, circle ‘R’ represents hard working, and circle ‘S’ represents tactful persons. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/ad7edd85-9599-48f0-8051-bb210ab1108b.png) How many urban persons are there who are educated and tactful but not hard working?
questions.explanation: Given: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/b7e7e932-df22-40ec-b716-2de61c85aea5.png) So, the number of urban persons who are educated and tactful but not hard working → 14 Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: HLOY is related to KNRA in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, TTAG is related to WVDI. To which of the given options is CZJM related, following the same logic?
questions.explanation: Given: HLOY is related to KNRA and TTAG is related to WVDI with same logic. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/777f7ed2-3704-4128-82d1-31ebce310849.png) Logic: Letters are increasing + 3 and + 2 place alternately. For, HLOY - KNRA H + 3 = K, L + 2 = N, O + 3 = R, Y + 2 = A For, TTAG - WVDI T + 3 = W, T + 2 = V, A + 3 = D, G + 2 = I Similarly, CZJM - ? C + 3 = F, Z + 2 = B, J + 3 = M, M + 2 = O So, CZJM is related to FBMO. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Riya starts from Point P and drives 8 km towards the south. Then she takes a right turn, drives 11 km, she turns right again and drives 14 km. She then takes a right turn and drives 15 km. Then she takes left turn and drives 2 km, she takes a left turn again and drives 4 km and stops at Point Q. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should she drive in order to reach Point P again? (All turns are 90-degree turns only unless specified.)
questions.explanation: Given: Riya starts from Point P and drives 8 km towards the south. Then she takes a right turn, drives 11 km, she turns right again and drives 14 km. She then takes a right turn and drives 15 km. Then she takes left turn and drives 2 km, she takes a left turn again and drives 4 km and stops at Point Q. From the given information path diagram will be. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/9eaf014c-a753-4502-9515-7cd999234d7a.png) 14 - 8 = 6km 6 + 2 = 8km He far 8km and towards South direction should she drive in order to reach Point P again. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation if ‘−’ and ‘÷’ are interchanged and ‘×’ and ‘+’ are interchanged? 19 + 40 − 8 × 2 ÷ 1 = ?
questions.explanation: Given: 19 + 40 − 8 × 2 ÷ 1 = ? Given Sign - × Interchanged Sign ÷ + Using BODMAS rule. ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[],{}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\ \hline \text{Addition} & + \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​ New equation: ​​19 × 40 ÷ 8 + 2 - 1 = ? 19 × 5 + 2 - 1 = ? 95 + 2 - 1 = ? 97 - 1 = ? ? = 96 Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: In the series given, some of the letters are missing, which are given in that order in one of the alternative. Choose the correct alternative. a _ ba _ bb _ ab _ a
questions.explanation: Information Given: Series: a _ ba _ bb _ ab _ a Options: abab, baab, baba, abbb Logic: Fill blanks and check for repeating pattern. Explanation: Logic: Check which option forms a consistent repeating block. (a) abab → aaba / bbba / abba ✘ no clear pattern (b) baab → abba / abba / abba ✔ consistent (c) baba → abba / abbb / abaa ✘ breaks pattern (d) abbb → aaba / bbbb / abba ✘ inconsistent Thus pattern = (abba)(abba)(abba) Final Answer: baab Final Correct Option: (b)

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: 1. All gardens are forests. 2. All forests are meadows. Conclusions: I. All gardens are meadows. II. All meadows are forests.
questions.explanation: ​Statements: 1. All gardens are forests. 2. All forests are meadows. From the given statements possible Venn diagram will be. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/4b55e483-c4c3-42a4-81af-e3467d4f9880.png) ​Conclusions: I. All gardens are meadows. (True, all gardens are forests and all forests are meadows, so that means all gardens are meadows). II. All meadows are forests. (False, all forests are meadows, so that means all meadows are not forests). So, ​Only conclusion (I) follows. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Eight people are sitting in two parallel rows containing 4 people each in such a way that there is equal distance between adwacent persons. In row 1, P, E, S and T are seated and all of them are facing south. In row 2, M, O, L and D are seated and all of them are facing north. Thus, each person faces another person from the other row. O faces the one sitting to the immediate right of P. Only one person sits between O and D. Only two people sit between L and D. D faces the one sitting second to the left of T. E sits at some place to the right of S. Which of the following represents both people sitting at extreme ends of the rows?
questions.explanation: Given - In row 1, P, E, S and T are seated and all of them are facing south. In row 2, M, O, L and D are seated and all of them are facing north. O faces the one sitting to the immediate right of P. Only one person sits between O and D. Only two people sit between L and D. D faces the one sitting second to the left of T. E sits at some place to the right of S. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/b895644f-79ea-4121-9595-31c7ac1d6bc7.png) E and D both people sitting at extreme ends of the rows?

questions.question: In this question, four versions of an office address are provided. Exactly two of them are completely identical. Identify those two and choose the correct option. 1. Surat Circle Second floor within premises Regional Post Office Ring Road Surat – 395002 2. Surat Circle Second floor within premises Regional Post Office Ring Road Surat – 395002 3. Surat Circle Second Floor within premises Regional Post Office Ring Road Surat – 395021 4. Surat Circle Second floor within campus Regional Post Office Ring Road Surat – 395002
questions.explanation: Information Given: Four versions of an office address are given. Task: Find exactly two addresses that are completely identical (word-for-word). Logic: Addresses must match in spelling, capitalization, wording, and PIN code. Explanation: Logic: Compare each line character-by-character. 1 and 2 → Completely identical (same words, same capitalization, same PIN). 3 → “Second Floor” (capital F) and PIN 395021 (different). 4 → Uses “within campus” instead of “within premises” (different). Short Trick: Check line-by-line: Floor case, premises/campus, and PIN code first. Final Answer: Addresses 1 and 2 are identical. Final Correct Option: (a)

questions.question: Which of the following options is the wrong figure in the series? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/5c801e0f-9f2a-43f4-9d1c-9473a59ba47a.png)
questions.explanation: Logic: The number of circles and stars goes on decreasing step by step in the series (for example: 9 → 8 → 7 …). Each number first appears with circles and then with stars. The figure in option D does not follow this decreasing pattern of circles and stars. Thus, the wrong figure is (D).

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘DONT’ is coded as ‘1097’ and ‘NATO’ is coded as ‘0187’. What is the code for ‘A’ in the given code language?
questions.explanation: Given: In a certain code language, ‘DONT’ is coded as ‘1097’ and ‘NATO’ is coded as ‘0187’. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/d3e7e238-6826-4c2b-9a2f-9beb081d1e0e.png) So, the code of A is 8. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 19, 42, 78, 122, 179, ?
questions.explanation: Given: 19, 42, 78, 122, 179, ? Logic: The differences between terms increase alternately by +13, +8, +13, +8... 42 − 19 = 23 78 − 42 = 36 122 − 78 = 44 179 − 122 = 57 Differences: 23, 36, 44, 57 Second-level differences: 36 − 23 = 13 44 − 36 = 8 57 − 44 = 13 Pattern alternates: +13, +8, +13, ... Next difference = 57 + 8 = 65 Next term = 179 + 65 = 244 So, the missing term is 244. Thus, correct option is (b).
